title: euonymus alatus var. rotundatus
intro: Euonymus alatus var. rotundatus, also known as the burning bush, is a deciduous shrub native to Asia, characterized by its unique branches that have a distinctive corky bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Euonymus alatus var. rotundatus include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and branches of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
